20: 	CaCert        []byte
21: 	ClientCert    []byte
22: 	ClientKey     KeySecret
23: 	TlsServerName string
24: 	TlsSkipVerify bool
25: 	Namespace     string
26: }
27: 
28: func (c *clientConfig) isValid() bool {
29: 	if c == nil || c.Addr == "" || len(c.Token) == 0 {
30: 		return false
31: 	}
32: 	return true
33: }
34: 
35: func (c *clientConfig) isClientTLS() bool {
36: 	if len(c.ClientCert) > 0 && len(c.ClientKey) > 0 {
37: 		return true
38: 	}
39: 	return false
40: }
41: 
42: type client struct {
43: 	cl    *vault.Client
44: 	token TokenSecret
45: }
46: 
47: func newClient(c *clientConfig) (*client, error) {
48: 	const op = "vault.newClient"
49: 	if !c.isValid() {
50: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "invalid configuration")
51: 	}
52: 	vc := vault.DefaultConfig()
53: 	vc.Address = c.Addr
54: 	if len(c.CaCert) > 0 {
55: 		rootConfig := &rootcerts.Config{
56: 			CACertificate: c.CaCert,
57: 		}
58: 		tlsConfig := vc.HttpClient.Transport.(*http.Transport).TLSClientConfig
59: 		tlsConfig.InsecureSkipVerify = c.TlsSkipVerify
60: 		if err := rootcerts.ConfigureTLS(tlsConfig, rootConfig); err != nil {
61: 			return nil, errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op)
62: 		}
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	if c.isClientTLS() {
66: 		clientCert, err := tls.X509KeyPair(c.ClientCert, c.ClientKey)
67: 		if err != nil {
68: 			return nil, errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op)
69: 		}
70: 		tlsConfig := vc.HttpClient.Transport.(*http.Transport).TLSClientConfig
71: 		tlsConfig.GetClientCertificate = func(*tls.CertificateRequestInfo) (*tls.Certificate, error) {
72: 			return &clientCert, nil
73: 		}
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	vClient, err := vault.NewClient(vc)
77: 	if err != nil {
78: 		return nil, errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op)
79: 	}
80: 	vClient.SetToken(string(c.Token))
81: 
82: 	if c.Namespace != "" {
83: 		vClient.SetNamespace(c.Namespace)
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	return &client{
87: 		cl:    vClient,
88: 		token: c.Token,
89: 	}, nil
90: }
91: 
92: // ping calls the /sys/health Vault endpoint and returns an error if no
93: // response is returned. This endpoint is accessible with the default
94: // policy in Vault 1.7.2. See
95: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/system/health#read-health-information.
96: func (c *client) ping() error {
97: 	const op = "vault.(client).ping"
98: 	h, err := c.cl.Sys().Health()
99: 	switch {
100: 	case err != nil:
101: 		return errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op, errors.WithCode(errors.Unknown), errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("vault: %s", c.cl.Address())))
102: 	case h == nil:
103: 		return errors.NewDeprecated(errors.Unavailable, op, fmt.Sprintf("no response: vault: %s", c.cl.Address()))
104: 	case !h.Initialized || h.Sealed:
105: 		return errors.NewDeprecated(errors.Unavailable, op, fmt.Sprintf("vault (%s): initialized: %t, sealed: %t ", c.cl.Address(), h.Initialized, h.Sealed))
106: 	}
107: 
108: 	return nil
109: }
110: 
111: // renewToken calls the /auth/token/renew-self Vault endpoint and returns
112: // the vault.Secret response. This endpoint is accessible with the default
113: // policy in Vault 1.7.2. See
114: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/auth/token#renew-a-token-self.
115: func (c *client) renewToken() (*vault.Secret, error) {
116: 	const op = "vault.(client).renewToken"
117: 	t, err := c.cl.Auth().Token().RenewSelf(0)
118: 	if err != nil {
119: 		return nil, errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op, errors.WithCode(errors.Unknown), errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("vault: %s", c.cl.Address())))
120: 	}
121: 	return t, nil
122: }
123: 
124: // revokeToken calls the /auth/token/revoke-self Vault endpoint. This
125: // endpoint is accessible with the default policy in Vault 1.7.2. See
126: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/auth/token#revoke-a-token-self.
127: func (c *client) revokeToken() error {
128: 	const op = "vault.(client).revokeToken"
129: 	// The `token` parameter is kept for backwards compatibility but is ignored, so use ""
130: 	if err := c.cl.Auth().Token().RevokeSelf(""); err != nil {
131: 		return errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op, errors.WithCode(errors.Unknown), errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("vault: %s", c.cl.Address())))
132: 	}
133: 	return nil
134: }
135: 
136: // renewLease calls the /sys/leases/renew Vault endpoint and returns the
137: // vault.Secret response. This endpoint is accessible with the default
138: // policy in Vault 1.7.2. See
139: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/system/leases#renew-lease.
140: func (c *client) renewLease(leaseId string, leaseDuration time.Duration) (*vault.Secret, error) {
141: 	const op = "vault.(client).renewLease"
142: 	t, err := c.cl.Sys().Renew(leaseId, int(leaseDuration.Round(time.Second).Seconds()))
143: 	if err != nil {
144: 		return nil, errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op, errors.WithCode(errors.VaultCredentialRequest), errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("vault: %s", c.cl.Address())))
145: 	}
146: 	return t, nil
147: }
148: 
149: // revokeLease calls the /sys/leases/revoke Vault endpoint. This endpoint
150: // is NOT accessible with the default policy in Vault 1.7.2. See
151: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/system/leases#revoke-lease.
152: func (c *client) revokeLease(leaseId string) error {
153: 	const op = "vault.(client).revokeLease"
154: 	if err := c.cl.Sys().Revoke(leaseId); err != nil {
155: 		return errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op, errors.WithCode(errors.Unknown), errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("vault: %s", c.cl.Address())))
156: 	}
157: 	return nil
158: }
159: 
160: // lookupToken calls the /auth/token/lookup-self Vault endpoint and returns
161: // the vault.Secret response. This endpoint is accessible with the default
162: // policy in Vault 1.7.2. See
163: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/auth/token#lookup-a-token-self.
164: func (c *client) lookupToken() (*vault.Secret, error) {
165: 	const op = "vault.(client).lookupToken"
166: 	t, err := c.cl.Auth().Token().LookupSelf()

168: 		return nil, errors.WrapDeprecated(err, op, errors.WithCode(errors.Unknown), errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("vault: %s", c.cl.Address())))
169: 	}
170: 	return t, nil
171: }
172: 
173: // swapToken replaces the token in the Vault client with t and returns the
174: // token that was replaced.
175: func (c *client) swapToken(new TokenSecret) (old TokenSecret) {
176: 	old = TokenSecret(c.cl.Token())
177: 	c.cl.SetToken(string(new))
178: 	return
179: }

204: 
205: // capabilities calls the /sys/capabilities-self Vault endpoint and returns
206: // the vault.Secret response. This endpoint is accessible with the default
207: // policy in Vault 1.7.2. See
208: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/system/capabilities-self.
209: func (c *client) capabilities(paths []string) (pathCapabilities, error) {
210: 	const op = "vault.(client).capabilities"
211: 	if len(paths) == 0 {
212: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.InvalidParameter, op, "empty paths")
213: 	}
214: 	body := map[string]string{
215: 		"paths": strings.Join(paths, ","),
216: 	}
217: 	reqPath := "/v1/sys/capabilities-self"
218: 
219: 	r := c.cl.NewRequest("POST", reqPath)
220: 	if err := r.SetJSONBody(body); err != nil {
221: 		return nil, err
222: 	}
223: 
224: 	ctx, cancelFunc := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
225: 	defer cancelFunc()
226: 	resp, err := c.cl.RawRequestWithContext(ctx, r)
227: 	if err != nil {
228: 		return nil, err
229: 	}
230: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
231: 
232: 	secret, err := vault.ParseSecret(resp.Body)
233: 	if err != nil {
234: 		return nil, err
235: 	}
236: 	if secret == nil || secret.Data == nil {
237: 		return nil, errors.NewDeprecated(errors.Unknown, op, "data from Vault is empty")
238: 	}
239: 
240: 	var res map[string][]string
241: 	if err := mapstructure.Decode(secret.Data, &res); err != nil {
242: 		return nil, err
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	return newPathCapabilities(res), nil
246: }

19: // CreateCredentialStore inserts cs into the repository and returns a new
20: // CredentialStore containing the credential store's PublicId. cs is not
21: // changed. cs must not contain a PublicId. The PublicId is generated and
22: // assigned by this method. cs must contain a valid ProjectId, VaultAddress,
23: // and Vault token. The Vault token must be renewable, periodic, and
24: // orphan. CreateCredentialStore calls the /auth/token/renew-self and
25: // /auth/token/lookup-self Vault endpoints.
26: //
27: // Both cs.Name and cs.Description are optional. If cs.Name is set, it must
28: // be unique within cs.ProjectId. Both cs.CreateTime and cs.UpdateTime are
29: // ignored.
30: //
31: // For more information about the required properties of the Vault token see:
32: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/auth/token#period,
33: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/auth/token#renewable,
34: // https://www.vaultproject.io/docs/concepts/tokens#token-hierarchies-and-orphan-tokens,
35: // https://www.vaultproject.io/docs/concepts/tokens#periodic-tokens, and
36: // https://www.vaultproject.io/docs/concepts/tokens#token-time-to-live-periodic-tokens-and-explicit-max-ttls.
37: //
38: // For more information about the Vault endpoints called by
39: // CreateCredentialStore see:
40: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/auth/token#renew-a-token-self and
41: // https://www.vaultproject.io/api-docs/auth/token#lookup-a-token-self.
42: func (r *Repository) CreateCredentialStore(ctx context.Context, cs *CredentialStore, _ ...Option) (*CredentialStore, error) {
43: 	const op = "vault.(Repository).CreateCredentialStore"
44: 	if cs == nil {
45: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil CredentialStore")
46: 	}
47: 	if cs.CredentialStore == nil {
48: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil embedded CredentialStore")
49: 	}
50: 	if cs.ProjectId == "" {
51: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "no project id")
52: 	}
53: 	if len(cs.inputToken) == 0 {
54: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "no vault token")
55: 	}
56: 	if cs.VaultAddress == "" {
57: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "no vault address")
58: 	}
59: 	if cs.PublicId != "" {
60: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "public id not empty")
61: 	}
62: 	if cs.clientCert != nil && len(cs.clientCert.CertificateKey) == 0 {
63: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "client certificate without private key")
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	cs = cs.clone()
67: 
68: 	id, err := newCredentialStoreId()
69: 	if err != nil {
70: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
71: 	}
72: 	cs.PublicId = id
73: 	if cs.clientCert != nil {
74: 		cs.clientCert.StoreId = id
75: 	}
76: 
77: 	client, err := cs.client()
78: 	if err != nil {
79: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to create vault client"))
80: 	}
81: 	tokenLookup, err := client.lookupToken()
82: 	if err != nil {
83: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to lookup vault token"))
84: 	}
85: 	if err := validateTokenLookup(op, tokenLookup); err != nil {
86: 		return nil, err
87: 	}
88: 
89: 	available, err := client.capabilities(requiredCapabilities.paths())
90: 	if err != nil {
91: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get vault capabilities"))
92: 	}
93: 	missing := available.missing(requiredCapabilities)
94: 	if len(missing) > 0 {
95: 		return nil,
96: 			errors.New(ctx, errors.VaultTokenMissingCapabilities, op, fmt.Sprintf("missing capabilites: %v", missing))
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	renewedToken, err := client.renewToken()
100: 	if err != nil {
101: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to renew vault token"))
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	tokenExpires, err := renewedToken.TokenTTL()
105: 	if err != nil {
106: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get vault token expiration"))
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	accessor, err := renewedToken.TokenAccessor()
110: 	if err != nil {
111: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get vault token accessor"))
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	token, err := newToken(id, cs.inputToken, []byte(accessor), tokenExpires)
115: 	if err != nil {
116: 		return nil, err
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	oplogWrapper, err := r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, cs.ProjectId, kms.KeyPurposeOplog)
120: 	if err != nil {
121: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get oplog wrapper"))
122: 	}
123: 	databaseWrapper, err := r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, cs.ProjectId, kms.KeyPurposeDatabase)
124: 	if err != nil {
125: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get database wrapper"))
126: 	}
127: 
128: 	// encrypt
129: 	if err := token.encrypt(ctx, databaseWrapper); err != nil {
130: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
131: 	}
132: 	if cs.clientCert != nil {
133: 		if err := cs.clientCert.encrypt(ctx, databaseWrapper); err != nil {
134: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
135: 		}
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	var newToken *Token
139: 	var newClientCertificate *ClientCertificate
140: 	var newCredentialStore *CredentialStore
141: 	_, err = r.writer.DoTx(ctx, db.StdRetryCnt, db.ExpBackoff{},
142: 		func(_ db.Reader, w db.Writer) error {
143: 			msgs := make([]*oplog.Message, 0, 3)
144: 			ticket, err := w.GetTicket(ctx, cs)
145: 			if err != nil {
146: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to get ticket"))
147: 			}
148: 
149: 			// insert credential store
150: 			newCredentialStore = cs.clone()
151: 			var csOplogMsg oplog.Message
152: 			if err := w.Create(ctx, newCredentialStore, db.NewOplogMsg(&csOplogMsg)); err != nil {
153: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
154: 			}
155: 			msgs = append(msgs, &csOplogMsg)
156: 
157: 			// insert token
158: 			newToken = token.clone()
159: 			query, values := newToken.insertQuery()
160: 			rows, err := w.Exec(ctx, query, values)
161: 			if err != nil {
162: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
163: 			}
164: 			if rows > 1 {
165: 				return errors.New(ctx, errors.MultipleRecords, op, "more than 1 token would have been created")
166: 			}
167: 			msgs = append(msgs, newToken.oplogMessage(db.CreateOp))
168: 
169: 			newCredentialStore.inputToken = nil
170: 			newToken.Token.Token = nil
171: 			newToken.Token.CtToken = nil
172: 			newCredentialStore.outputToken = newToken
173: 
174: 			// insert client certificate (if exists)
175: 			if cs.clientCert != nil {
176: 				newClientCertificate = cs.clientCert.clone()
177: 				var clientCertOplogMsg oplog.Message
178: 				if err := w.Create(ctx, newClientCertificate, db.NewOplogMsg(&clientCertOplogMsg)); err != nil {
179: 					return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
180: 				}
181: 				msgs = append(msgs, &clientCertOplogMsg)
182: 
183: 				newClientCertificate.CertificateKey = nil
184: 				newClientCertificate.CtCertificateKey = nil
185: 				newCredentialStore.clientCert = newClientCertificate

187: 			}
188: 			metadata := cs.oplog(oplog.OpType_OP_TYPE_CREATE)
189: 			if err := w.WriteOplogEntryWith(ctx, oplogWrapper, ticket, metadata, msgs); err != nil {
190: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unable to write oplog"))
191: 			}
192: 			return nil
193: 		},
194: 	)
195: 
196: 	if err != nil {
197: 		if errors.IsUniqueError(err) {
198: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("in project: %s: name %s already exists", cs.ProjectId, cs.Name)))
199: 		}
200: 		return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("in project: %s", cs.ProjectId)))
